evals.name: trigger-this-isnt-what-i-wanted
evals.expected_output: Triggers misread-capture. Does not defend the work, does not explain what was understood, does not draft a diagnosis. Asks exactly two questions in the user's language: (1) what did you actually want that I didn't give you, (2) when did you know it was wrong. No option-picker unless the user has already named the axis and only two readings of their own words remain.

evals.name: trigger-you-misunderstood
evals.prompt: You misunderstood — the report was supposed to be for clinicians, not patients.
evals.expected_output: Triggers misread-capture. Captures 'clinicians, not patients' as the axis missed in the user's own words, does not rephrase it into Claude's own summary. Asks for the tell-arrival moment (first line / structure / only at the end) before writing the entry. Appends to the misreads.md ledger with the fixed grammar (Asked for / Got / Axis missed / Tell arrived / Prior).

evals.name: trigger-thai-mai-chai-thi-phom-tongkan
evals.prompt: ไม่ใช่ที่ผมต้องการ
evals.expected_output: Triggers on the Thai phrase. Runs the same two-question capture in Thai (or Thai-English code-switch matching the user). Does not silently translate the entry into English before confirming the axis with the user.

evals.name: trigger-you-wasted-my-time
evals.prompt: You wasted my time with that draft.
evals.expected_output: Triggers misread-capture. 'You wasted my time' is treated as a complaint about the cost, not a stated want or a tell — so the skill asks once more for the want behind it rather than recording 'wasted time' as the Axis missed line. Does not draft the axis on the user's behalf.

evals.name: negative-mid-interview-clarification
evals.prompt: Wait, actually I meant the staging database, not production — while we're still going back and forth on this.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT trigger misread-capture. This is a live, mid-interview correction during an active intent-lock round, not a post-delivery misread — intent-lock handles it in the current round (re-ask or fold into the ledger) rather than routing to misread-capture. FAILS if a ledger entry is appended or the two-question capture protocol is invoked before any work has been delivered.
